content: Hi, I'm thinking of renovating my home in preparation for a move to Taiwan. I've been learning Spanish, but I'm switching to Mandarin to prepare for the move.
That's a big change! Renovating your home before a move can be a great way to get everything in order. What kind of changes are you thinking of making to your home?
I'd like to add some more storage and maybe a new room to my house. I've been collecting books on rock climbing and urban sketching, and I need a place to store them.
A new room sounds like a big project! Have you considered hiring a professional to help with the construction?
Yeah, I've been thinking about it. I've got a friend, Michael, who's a contractor, and he's offered to help me out. But I'm not sure if I want to spend the money on a full-scale renovation.
It's great that you have a friend who can help. You might want to consider getting a few quotes from different contractors to compare prices and services.

That's a good idea. I've also been thinking about adding some origami decorations to my home. I've always been fascinated by the art of origami, and I think it would be a great way to add some personality to my space.
Origami is a beautiful art form! You might want to consider taking a class or workshop to learn more about it. What's your timeline looking like for this renovation?
I'm hoping to have everything done by the end of May, so I can focus on my Mandarin studies over the summer. I've got a trip planned to Taiwan in September, and I want to make sure I'm prepared.
That's a good deadline to work towards. Make sure to also prioritize your Mandarin studies and not get too overwhelmed with the renovation. Are you planning on moving to Taiwan permanently?
Not permanently, but I'm thinking of spending a few years there to immerse myself in the language and culture. I've got a friend, David, who's living in Taipei, and he's offered to show me around.

That sounds like an amazing opportunity! Immersion is a great way to learn a language. You might want to consider packing some of your favorite stand-up comedy books to take with you, so you can practice your Mandarin with locals.
Ha! That's a great idea. I'll have to make sure to pack my favorite books, like those by Jerry Seinfeld and Ellen DeGeneres. Maybe I can even find some new comedians to enjoy while I'm there.
You can definitely find some new comedians to enjoy in Taiwan. If you need any more recommendations or help with your renovation, feel free to ask.
Will do, thanks! I'm also thinking of starting a small garden in my backyard. Do you have any tips on getting started with gardening?

Gardening is a great hobby! There are many online resources and tutorials that can help you get started. You might want to try growing some easy-to-care-for plants like herbs or succulents to begin with.
That sounds like a good idea. I'll have to check out some tutorials on YouTube. Maybe I can even grow some plants to use in my origami decorations.
That's a great idea! Growing your own plants can be a fun and rewarding experience. If you need any more help or have any other questions, don't hesitate to ask.
Thanks, I will! I'm also planning on hosting a dinner party at my place on Friday, June 16th, and I want to make sure I have everything set up and ready to go.

That sounds like a blast! Make sure to test out your recipes and equipment beforehand to ensure everything is working smoothly. If you need any help with setting up or have any other questions, I'm here to help.

Will do, thanks! I'm excited to get started on my renovation and my Mandarin studies. I'm planning on spending about $10,000 on the renovation, and I'm hoping to have it all done by the end of August.
That sounds like a plan! Make sure to stay on track with your budget and timeline, and don't hesitate to reach out if you need any more help or have any other questions.
